module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand charge management
learningObjectives.description: Explain transportation pricing logic and how charge management supports settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Understand charge calculation flow
learningObjectives.description: Describe agreement determination, sheet application, and charge validation sequence.

learningObjectives.title: Understand pricing factors
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how distance, weight, mode, surcharges, and service levels affect cost.

sections.content: Charge Management is the SAP TM functionality responsible for calculating transportation costs. It determines freight charges using predefined charge calculation sheets, freight agreements, rate tables and calculation rules. The calculated charges are later transferred to Freight Settlement for carrier payment.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Charge Calculation determines HOW MUCH transportation costs before Freight Settlement pays the carrier.

sections.title: Pricing Factors
rows: Road, Rail, Air or Ocean rates

sections.content: A shipment weighing 12 tons travels 650 kilometers by road. SAP TM retrieves the carrier's freight agreement, applies the charge calculation sheet, calculates the base freight, fuel surcharge and toll charges, then passes the total amount to Freight Settlement.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain standardized Charge Calculation Sheets and regularly update freight agreements to ensure accurate pricing and settlement.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A manufacturing company automated charge calculations for more than 18,000 monthly shipments. Freight pricing accuracy improved significantly while manual pricing effort was almost eliminated.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember the sequence: Freight Agreement -> Charge Calculation Sheet -> Charge Calculation -> Freight Settlement Document. This sequence is frequently tested in SAP TM certification exams.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Charge Calculation only computes transportation costs. It does not post accounting entries or make carrier payments—that occurs during Freight Settlement.

items: Charge Calculation uses freight agreements and pricing rules.
Multiple charge components contribute to the final freight cost.
Calculated charges are transferred to Freight Settlement.
Accurate charge configuration ensures correct carrier payments.
